description: Tracks schema changes per kryptonite version. Used by the validator to understand which fields existed at a given version and by migration logic to upgrade older epics.

changes.description: Phase 3 architecture decisions captured as ADRs. Same shape as spec-schema.json's architecture.decisions[], plus extension fields source_phase and related_stories[] for traceability. Lifted into spec.json.architecture.decisions[] verbatim during Phase 10 (the generator drops the extension fields).
Phase 3 unresolved gaps captured as OQs. Same shape as spec-schema.json's open_questions[], plus extension field source_phase. Lifted into spec.json.open_questions[] verbatim during Phase 10.
Phase 6 append-only log of scope deltas after spike findings. Each entry records phase, trigger, and added/removed/modified/deferred story IDs.
New JSON schema defining the full epic.json shape. Replaces the inline field list previously in storage-protocol.md (which had drifted from reality).

Phase 7 output. Was a free-form object with minProperties: 1 — now structured under testing/non_functional/infrastructure/patterns. Phase 7 gate (on 0.6.0+ epics) requires at least one of the four sub-objects to be populated.

Phase 8 output. Now a structured object (nav, header, layout, colors, typography, spacing, components) instead of free-form prose. Phase 8 gate (on 0.6.0+ epics) requires shell_summary to be populated whenever design_direction.locked === true so detail-mock Designers inherit the visual DNA deterministically.

Version-suffixed supplemental gate schemas. validate-gate.js loads them on top of the base gate when epic.kryptonite_version >= 0.6.0. Older epics keep using the base gate alone — no retroactive enforcement.

Validator now loads version-suffixed supplemental gate schemas (e.g. 03.0.6.0.json) on top of the base gate when the epic's kryptonite_version is at or above the suffix. Errors from supplementals are labeled SCHEMA (vX.Y.Z+) for clarity.

Canonicalizes the per-story mock working-data the Phase 8 mock workflow already writes into state.json. mock_path (string, repo-relative path to the approved mock HTML — already emitted by agents/designer.md's Report Format and already allowed by wave-gate-report-schema.json), mock_options (array of option identifiers shown in /compare, e.g. ['a','b','c']), mock_choice (string, the selected option), design_notes (string, free-form notes on the chosen content-area treatment, inherited by detail Designers and the spec generator). BUGFIX: story-schema.json sets additionalProperties:false, so before this entry these four fields failed the Phase 9 gate even though the skill's own designer agent produces them — an internal inconsistency. No migration needed: the fields were already present in 0.6.0 epics' state.json and are now simply permitted.

notes: 0.5.0 epics resume cleanly. The new gates apply only to epics created on 0.6.0+ — the validator keys off epic.kryptonite_version, not the installed plugin version.
An in-flight 0.5.0 epic finishes Phase 8 on its current shape. To bring an old epic onto the 0.6.0 schema, follow the optional migration mapping below.
steps: If the epic has a sidecar gap_analysis.md (or equivalent Phase 3 notes), translate each confirmed gap-probe resolution into an ADR under epic.json.decisions[] (id ADR-001+, status 'accepted', context/choice/consequences/alternatives per references/epic-schema.json). Translate each unresolved gap into an OQ under epic.json.open_questions[].
If the epic has a sidecar rescope.md, translate each scope delta into an entry under epic.json.scope_history[] with phase, trigger, and added/removed/modified/deferred arrays.

If the epic has a sidecar technical_guidance.md, translate the test approach into technical_context.testing.{backend,frontend,e2e}; non-functional sections into technical_context.non_functional.*; infrastructure integrations into technical_context.infrastructure.{integrate_with,do_not_integrate,gating}; architectural patterns into technical_context.patterns.{honor,avoid}.

If foundational mocks are approved, write the design system summary into epic.json.design_direction.shell_summary as a structured object (nav, header, layout, colors, typography, spacing, components).
Bump epic.json.kryptonite_version to '0.6.0'. The next phase gate run will exercise the new supplemental schemas.
Rename any migrated sidecar markdowns to *.legacy.md so the interviewer doesn't re-read them as authoritative — they're paper trail only.

changes.description: Required array of user journeys per wave. Each journey has id (UJ-NNN), name, stories_covered[], and steps[] (Chrome MCP actions). Used by wave-uat-agent.
Optional top-level config to toggle individual gates and tune max_fix_attempts and fix_strategies.

Optional structured block: start_command, stop_command, health_check, app_url, ready_signal, startup_timeout_ms. Drives service lifecycle in Phase B. Without it, UAT/UX gates skip for that repo's stories.
ISO timestamp of when the story branch was merged into the wave branch.
Per-story validation fields dropped — wave-level gates replace per-story QA/Reviewer/CodeReview agents.

From [pending, in_progress, qa_validation, in_review, done, blocked, cancelled, deferred] to [pending, in_progress, merged, done, blocked, cancelled, deferred]. Story is 'merged' after its branch lands in the wave branch; promotes to 'done' when the wave completes.

Replaces freeform spec.html. Validated by references/spec-schema.json. Composes story-schema.json via $ref. Contains overview, parties, repos, architecture (components/interactions/decisions), data_model, api_boundaries, nfrs, technical_constraints, design_direction, stories, spike_findings, open_questions.
Replaces freeform plan.html. Validated by references/plan-schema.json. Contains summary, waves (with parallel_groups, tasks, demo_checkpoint, user_journeys), critical_path, parallel_strategy, risks.

Wave gate reports have status [pass, fail, blocked]. Blocked = infrastructure unavailable; orchestrator pauses for user instead of entering fix loop. Issues[].severity gains 'blocked' alongside critical/high/medium/low.
Immutable history of wave gate attempts. Each entry has uat/ux/spec_compliance/code_review status + report_path, plus issues[] with fix_attempts[] for adaptive retry tracking.

steps: BREAKING: 0.4.0 only supports the wave-gate execution model. Projects mid-execution under 0.3.0 will not run on 0.4.0 — finish them on 0.3.0 or restart Phase 12 under 0.4.0.
Update any story with status 'qa_validation', 'in_review', or 'code_review' to 'merged' (or 'in_progress' if work hasn't started). The new schema rejects the old values.
Drop the per-story fields dod_validation, review_status, code_review_status, qa_status if present — they are no longer recognized.
Convert spec.html to spec.json (validated against references/spec-schema.json) and plan.html to plan.json (validated against references/plan-schema.json). The new file format is required.
Add a user_journeys[] array to each wave in plan.json with at least one journey covering the wave's stories — the plan validator rejects waves without it.
Recommended: add a 'testing' block to each repo in repos.json that needs a running service. Without it, UAT and UX gates skip for that repo (with warnings).

changes.description: Static frontend: DaisyUI + Tailwind + Alpine.js dark mode UI with Overview, Stories (kanban), Mocks (gallery+compare), Dashboard pages
API endpoint returning epic.json content
API endpoint returning repos.json as array
Epic switcher — list all epics and switch active context without restart
Migrated from flat array to { spec_comments: [], story_comments: [] } with threading (parent_id) and resolve toggle
Phase 12 semantic check: stories in an in_progress wave must not be 'pending' or missing status

steps: comments.json: if file is a plain array, it becomes { spec_comments: <array>, story_comments: [] } — handled automatically on load
Stories in active waves should have status updated from 'pending' to 'in_progress' when wave execution begins

changes.description: Version stamp for migration tracking
Two-phase mock system: foundational vs detail
Structured design system summary from foundational mocks
Records which mock phase locked the direction
List of story IDs classified as foundational mocks
List of story IDs classified as detail mocks
Phase gate validation schemas (01-12)

steps: For stories with has_mock: true, add mock_phase: 'detail' (safe default — assumes direction was already established)
